releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Local LLM Support (OpenAI-compatible)

The local supporting any OpenAI-compatible backend — Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, and more.

- Optional API key via `LOCAL_LLM_API_KEY` env var (desktop) or iOS Keychain promp to improve safety
- Tested with LM Studio with/without API key
- Tested model: glm-ocr Q8

#6 

closes #5

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Recommended local model: glm-ocr:bf16 (via Ollama)

If you want free, offline PDF/image recognition without any API key, GLM-OCR is the best option I've tested:

Speed: ~6 seconds per page
VRAM: ~4.8 GB (fits on an RTX 2060 or equivalent)
Cost: completely free, runs locally via Ollama
Setup:
```
ollama pull glm-ocr:bf16
```
Then in plugin settings:

Model → Ollama (Local)
Ollama Model → glm-ocr:bf16
Recognition Prompt → Text Recognition:

**Fix**
- Reduce default PDF rendering DPI from 200 to 150 to stay within
input size limits for local models.
- Update the recognition prompt to suppress unnecessary code fences
and improve table/structure transcription instructions.

releases.author: kkbin505
releases.description:
## v 0.1.7

### New Features

** Image File Support**
Right-click any PNG/JPG/JPEG/WebP image in the file browser → "Convert Image to Markdown".

** In-Note Image Conversion**
Right-click images embedded inside open notes → result is inserted directly below the image, no new file created.

releases.author: kkbin505
releases.description:
Initial release of PDF2MD Obsidian plugin

Features:

Convert handwritten PDFs to Markdown with AI
Support for Qwen (recommended, cheapest) and OpenAI models
Intelligent file conflict resolution
API key security with masking and environment variable support
Real-time progress tracking with visual progress bar
Configurable DPI, timeout, and retry settings
Supported Models:

Qwen VL Max (¥0.00345/page, recommended)
OpenAI GPT
Gemini 
Installation:

In Obsidian: Settings → Community Plugins → Search "PDF2MD"
Click Install and Enable
Configure API key in plugin settings
Right-click PDF → "Convert to Markdown"
releases.name: pdf to markdown v 0.1.0
